| [index.html](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/index.html "index.html") | [index.html](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/index.html "index.html") | [security: address all 10 findings from external audit (issue](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/commit/a68c6852d843ded0118f0a289dcbe3e8eebd7a39 "security: address all 10 findings from external audit (issue #476)  Implements VT-001 through VT-010 from the 2026-07-10 external security audit (Trivy/Semgrep/ZAP/Gitleaks/pip-audit against a real deployment):  - VT-001 (Critical): AST hardening in backtest/runner.py rejects   network/subprocess/eval/os.environ/unsafe-open calls reachable from   generated SignalEngine method bodies (previously only checked   top-level statements). Defense-in-depth: ephemeral per-run HOME,   RLIMIT_AS/NOFILE caps, best-effort UID-drop to a new vibe-sandbox   user (currently inert under Docker's cap model - see session doc;   accepted as documented residual gap, primary AST defense unaffected). - VT-002: multi-stage Dockerfile split so build-essential never   reaches the runtime image. - VT-003: SSE auth moved from a long-lived API key in the URL query   string to short-lived single-use tickets; access-log redaction for   any remaining query secrets. - VT-004: agent/.env split-brain fixed (bind mount + atomic 0600   writes) between docker-compose and Web UI settings writes. - VT-005: /correlation now requires auth + per-IP rate limit; generic   exceptions no longer leak raw text to clients. - VT-006: CSP-Report-Only + security headers middleware; Google Fonts   replaced with self-hosted @fontsource packages. - VT-007: docker-compose hardening (cap_drop ALL + minimal cap_add,   no-new-privileges, read_only rootfs, resource limits). - VT-008: /health split into /live (liveness) and /ready (real   provider-config readiness check); /health kept as a compat alias. - VT-009: base images pinned by digest, GitHub Actions pinned by   commit SHA, dependabot.yml added, hash-verified requirements-lock.txt   now actually wired into the Docker build via --require-hashes. - VT-010: universe-panel pickle cache sidecar upgraded from an   unkeyed SHA-256 (forgeable by anyone with cache-dir write access)   to a keyed HMAC.  Implemented via a 5-parcel Workflow (file-disjoint, independent adversarial verification per parcel, then a holistic final audit). The final audit caught a real regression (CI env-var-gate violation) and confirmed a dependency-pinning gap, both fixed and re-verified here, including a latent transitive-dependency bug the fix surfaced (smartmoneyconcepts<0.0.27 pulls an unbuildable zigzag package) - see docs/sessions/2026-07-13_session_vt476_security_hardening.md (local, gitignored) for full evidence trail.  Full suite: 5191 passed, 6 pre-existing/environmental failures (unchanged from main). Docker build + hardened compose boot verified end-to-end.") [#476](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/issues/476) [)](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/commit/a68c6852d843ded0118f0a289dcbe3e8eebd7a39 "security: address all 10 findings from external audit (issue #476)  Implements VT-001 through VT-010 from the 2026-07-10 external security audit (Trivy/Semgrep/ZAP/Gitleaks/pip-audit against a real deployment):  - VT-001 (Critical): AST hardening in backtest/runner.py rejects   network/subprocess/eval/os.environ/unsafe-open calls reachable from   generated SignalEngine method bodies (previously only checked   top-level statements). Defense-in-depth: ephemeral per-run HOME,   RLIMIT_AS/NOFILE caps, best-effort UID-drop to a new vibe-sandbox   user (currently inert under Docker's cap model - see session doc;   accepted as documented residual gap, primary AST defense unaffected). - VT-002: multi-stage Dockerfile split so build-essential never   reaches the runtime image. - VT-003: SSE auth moved from a long-lived API key in the URL query   string to short-lived single-use tickets; access-log redaction for   any remaining query secrets. - VT-004: agent/.env split-brain fixed (bind mount + atomic 0600   writes) between docker-compose and Web UI settings writes. - VT-005: /correlation now requires auth + per-IP rate limit; generic   exceptions no longer leak raw text to clients. - VT-006: CSP-Report-Only + security headers middleware; Google Fonts   replaced with self-hosted @fontsource packages. - VT-007: docker-compose hardening (cap_drop ALL + minimal cap_add,   no-new-privileges, read_only rootfs, resource limits). - VT-008: /health split into /live (liveness) and /ready (real   provider-config readiness check); /health kept as a compat alias. - VT-009: base images pinned by digest, GitHub Actions pinned by   commit SHA, dependabot.yml added, hash-verified requirements-lock.txt   now actually wired into the Docker build via --require-hashes. - VT-010: universe-panel pickle cache sidecar upgraded from an   unkeyed SHA-256 (forgeable by anyone with cache-dir write access)   to a keyed HMAC.  Implemented via a 5-parcel Workflow (file-disjoint, independent adversarial verification per parcel, then a holistic final audit). The final audit caught a real regression (CI env-var-gate violation) and confirmed a dependency-pinning gap, both fixed and re-verified here, including a latent transitive-dependency bug the fix surfaced (smartmoneyconcepts<0.0.27 pulls an unbuildable zigzag package) - see docs/sessions/2026-07-13_session_vt476_security_hardening.md (local, gitignored) for full evidence trail.  Full suite: 5191 passed, 6 pre-existing/environmental failures (unchanged from main). Docker build + hardened compose boot verified end-to-end.") | last weekJul 12, 2026 |

| [tailwind.config.ts](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/tailwind.config.ts "tailwind.config.ts") | [tailwind.config.ts](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/tailwind.config.ts "tailwind.config.ts") | [feat(i18n): add ja/ko/ar locales, RTL layout, LanguageSwitcher, and i…](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/commit/1e8fd7063223cab2e8c7860c04819ed760b6637a "feat(i18n): add ja/ko/ar locales, RTL layout, LanguageSwitcher, and i18n tests  Add Japanese, Korean, and Arabic locales to match the README set, replace the two-option zh/en toggle with a full language dropdown, and add comprehensive i18n parity tests.  i18n core - New ja/ko/ar.json (634 keys each, 100% parity with en.json). - SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES registry + isRtl()/applyDocumentDirection()   helpers; navigator as secondary detector with Node.js 21+ guard;   nonExplicitSupportedLngs for regional variant matching. - New i18next.d.ts for strict t() key type-checking at compile time.  Components - LanguageSwitcher dropdown in Layout.tsx: position: fixed + JS-measured   coords (resists overflow/clipping/RTL direction), full ARIA, touchstart   + Escape dismiss, i18n.languages array for regional code matching. - WelcomeScreen: fix double-prefix bug — CATEGORIES/CAPABILITY keys now   carry full i18n paths with `as const`. - AlphaZoo: 10 dynamic t() calls use `as any` + defaultValue fallback. - APP_VERSION constant removed; version string sourced from t('app.version').  RTL & theme - Logical properties throughout (border-e, ps/pe, border-s-2) +   rtl:flex-row-reverse on main layout. - --popover CSS variable defined (light + dark); bg-popover was producing   no color before, making every popover transparent. - [dir=\"rtl\"] .rtl\:flip-x rule for mirroring directional icons.  Tests (i18n.test.ts — 231 total, all passing) - Key parity: 5 locales × 2 directions (missing + extra) + sanity check. - Interpolation variable parity across all locales. - isRtl: base codes, regional variants (ar-EG/ar-SA), unknown codes. - SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES registry integrity.  zh-CN.json - Translation refinements (\"A股回测\"→\"多市场回测\", description alignment   with en.json); key ordering aligned to en.json structure.  Signed-off-by: digger yu <digger-yu@outlook.com>") | 3 weeks agoJun 28, 2026 |
| [tsconfig.json](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/tsconfig.json "tsconfig.json") | [tsconfig.json](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/tsconfig.json "tsconfig.json") | [feat(loaders,i18n): add baostock/tencent A-share sources + zh-CN fron…](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/commit/2594d83f8923702385c650d3e2ec37c43188227b "feat(loaders,i18n): add baostock/tencent A-share sources + zh-CN frontend i18n  A-share data sources (free, no-auth, bypass eastmoney CDN IP blocks): - baostock loader (TCP protocol) + tencent loader (HTTP), both wrapped with   cached_loader_fetch like the mootdx/akshare loaders so they honor the opt-in   local cache - registered into the a_share fallback chain (tushare -> mootdx -> baostock ->   tencent -> akshare); auto-detect default source stays tushare - pyproject [ashare] extra for the optional baostock dependency  Frontend i18n (English default, sidebar toggle to Chinese, persisted): - i18next + react-i18next + browser-languagedetector; first visit defaults to   English (localStorage-only detection, no navigator sniff), the toggle persists   the choice across reloads - en / zh-CN locales at full key parity, including the Runtime page - i18n initialized in the test setup so useTranslation resolves real strings;   restored the exported METRIC_LABELS so formatters consumers/tests keep working  Thanks @skloxo (#217) for the i18n layer and the A-share data sources.  Co-authored-by: skloxo <skloxo@users.noreply.github.com>") | last monthJun 13, 2026 |

| [vitest.config.ts](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/vitest.config.ts "vitest.config.ts") | [vitest.config.ts](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/blob/main/frontend/vitest.config.ts "vitest.config.ts") | [test: add frontend test suite + backend security/coverage tests](https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading/commit/16e22b97253de1ed9e453c830e96f14094d4876b "test: add frontend test suite + backend security/coverage tests  Cherry-picked the test-infra commit from #175 (sambazhu): vitest setup + 19 frontend test files (197 tests), backend auth/path-traversal/CORS tests, yfinance crypto fallback (BTC-USDT->BTC-USD), and CI coverage + frontend-test steps. Dropped the PR's separate i18n commit to stay consistent with #163.  Fix: align @vitest/coverage-v8 to ^3.2.6 (was ^4.1.8, incompatible with vitest ^3.2.6 - blocked npm install and the new npm ci CI step).") | last monthJun 5, 2026 |
